title: Tech Stack Applications
headings: Tech Stack Process Has Now Moved to HelpLab
Planning to Create a New Merge Request?
text:
Tech Stack Applications

Tech Stack Process Has Now Moved to HelpLab

The entire Tech Stack management process has officially transitioned to HelpLab on Wednesday, October 22nd .

Tech Stack on HelpLab is located here . It’s also available via the “Application Tech Stack” app in Okta.

Have an existing Merge Request?

No need to worry. Depending on the progress, we will complete and replicate the change onto Tech Stack on HelpLab .

Planning to Create a New Merge Request?

Please use the new Tech Stack Update Form instead of submitting an MR:

Use the Tech Stack Update Form in HelpLab:

Okta > HelpLab > IT > Tech Stack Update

Definition and Access

The Tech Stack is a list of all the technology that GitLab currently uses to support the business. It includes systems, applications, tools, and data used across all departments, along with details such as the business purpose/description, owners, provisioners, access teams, and other relevant attributes.

The Tech Stack in HelpLab is now the official single source of truth for all system information and changes. You can access it through:

Okta → HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update

By searching for the “Application Tech Stack” app in Okta Dashboard.

Direct link: Tech Stack on HelpLab

Many applications have a Tech Stack Guide to help you understand how each app is used and implemented.

This Jupyter Notebook view of the Tech Stack allows filtering and exporting CSV, JSON, or Markdown versions of the data.

Roles and Responsibilities

Business and Technical Owners of Systems

This Jupyter Notebook view of the Tech Stack allows filtering and exporting CSV, JSON, or Markdown versions of the data.

Roles and Responsibilities

Business and Technical Owners of Systems

Accountable for completeness and accuracy of Tech Stack data; Process Tech Stack updates ; Primary contacts for system-related inquiries and administration.

IT Business Technology

Code Owners of Tech Stack; Advise Business and Technical Owners in supporting systems.

Help facilitate Tech Stack updates, including the Business Impact Analysis for new and existing systems.

Tech Stack Definitions

Business Owner : The Business Owner is the individual(s) responsible for all budget and decision making around the tool. They should define how the tool is used and by whom. This person(s) usually has login access to the tool as Owner but login access isn’t necessary in all cases. Please make sure you list individual people in this field, rather than teams.

title: Tech Stack Applications
headings: Tech Stack Process Has Now Moved to HelpLab
Planning to Create a New Merge Request?
text:
the individual(s) responsible for all budget and decision making around the tool. They should define how the tool is used and by whom. This person(s) usually has login access to the tool as Owner but login access isn’t necessary in all cases. Please make sure you list individual people in this field, rather than teams.

Technical Owner : This may be the same as the Business Owner based on the nature of the system, for instance, if the system isn’t centrally managed and or/requires little to no configuration. For systems that are centrally managed and/or heavily configured, the Technical Owner is someone with understanding of the application’s architecture, functionality (including types of data involved), and overall criticality/impact to GitLab. Technical Owner(s) are typically administrators of a tool. This includes everyone with the administrative clearance to provision and deprovision access of a tool and/or as the technical expertise needed to manage it.

Provisioner/Deprovisioner : People in charge of granting and removing team member access to a tool. At least two provisioners/deprovisioners should be named for every tool in the handbook. Provisioners can be contacted as a group in Slack using the @provisioners handle and in GitLab using the @tech-stack-provisioner handle.

All Tech Stack changes must now be submitted using the Tech Stack Update Form in HelpLab.

How to Submit a Tech Stack Update Request

Go to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update (Okta path: Okta → HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update)

How to Submit a Tech Stack Update Request

Go to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update (Okta path: Okta → HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update)

Go to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update (Okta path: Okta → HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update)

In HelpLab, open the Tech Stack Update form under the IT section.

In HelpLab, open the Tech Stack Update form under the IT section.

Once the form loads, select the relevant option under “What can we help you with?” The applicable follow-up questions will automatically appear.

Once the form loads, select the relevant option under “What can we help you with?” The applicable follow-up questions will automatically appear.

Fill in all required details and submit the request.

Fill in all required details and submit the request.

Processing & Workflow

All requests will be routed through HelpLab.

The HelpLab Team / IT EntApps Team will review, validate, and update entries accordingly.

Ownership roles remain unchanged — you will still identify the Business Owner, Technical Owner, and Provisioners for each system.

What data lives in the Tech Stack?

Please ensure that whenever you update the tech stack, you follow the instructions below and use the right type of data in each field.

What data lives in the Tech Stack?

Please ensure that whenever you update the tech stack, you follow the instructions below and use the right type of data in each field.

Responsible for filling out information

The name of the system you are adding to the tech stack. Please enter the full and correct brand name associated with the tool. Example: “ Zendesk , not ZenDesk.”

MR Author and contributors

team_member_baseline_entitlement

A baseline entitlement is a system that ALL GitLab Team Members get access to. Example: “Zoom”. Most systems in our Tech Stack are not baseline entitlements.

MR Author and contributors

Business Purpose of the system. Please add links to handbook pages or websites that can provide people with more information on what the system is used for. Example: “ ContractWorks is a contract managing software. This process is used to file contract or related vendor documents after they are fully executed.”

MR Author and contributors

Define which individuals or teams need access to this system. Example: Strategic Marketing and Product Managers

MR Author and contributors

Text in single quotes, GitLab Username

Add the username of the people in charge of provisioning access to this system, separated by commas. We require at least 2 people to be listed as provisioners of a tool. Example: ‘ @username , @username1 ’

MR Author and contributors

Text in single quotes, GitLab Username

Text (Tier 1 Mission Critical, Tier 2 Business Critical, Tier 3 Business Operational, Tier 4 Administrative, TBD) or Unknown**

This field classifies the system based on GitLab’s Critical System Tier Definitions . The assignment of a critical system tier is dependent on the completion of a Business Impact Analysis (BIA) questionnaire. The Security Risk Team will coordinate the completion of a BIA if it has not yet been completed at the time a system is being added to the Tech Stack.

Adding a New System to the Tech Stack

The process for adding a new system to the tech stack has fully moved to HelpLab. Instead of creating a merge request in the tech_stack.yml file, follow the process below:

Go to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update (Okta path: Okta → HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update)

Go to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update (Okta path: Okta → HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update)

Updating an Existing System in Tech Stack

All system updates must now be made through HelpLab. Instead of creating a merge request in the tech_stack.yml file, follow the process below:

Navigate to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update and select Update an Existing System .

Choose the system you want to modify.

text:
inalized.

Updating an Existing System in Tech Stack

All system updates must now be made through HelpLab. Instead of creating a merge request in the tech_stack.yml file, follow the process below:

Navigate to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update and select Update an Existing System .

Choose the system you want to modify.

The form will automatically display follow-up questions based on the type of update.

Provide the necessary changes and submit the form.

The appropriate reviewer groups will validate and process the update as part of the HelpLab workflow.

Removing a System from Tech Stack

To remove a system from the tech stack, use the Tech Stack Update form instead of submitting a merge request to the tech_stack.yml file.

Go to HelpLab → IT → Tech Stack Update and select Remove a System .

Provide context for the deprecation or offboarding, along with any required details to initiate the process.

As part of this workflow, the Business Owner must coordinate with Legal and IT Compliance to ensure data deletion and contractual requirements are fully addressed.

Once all steps are completed and verified, the HelpLab Team / EntApps Team will remove the system from the tech stack.

Occasionally, systems listed in our tech stack will be deprecated. If a system is being offboarded (no longer used or being replaced), please submit a Remove a System request through the Tech Stack Update workflow in HelpLab.

ted and verified, the HelpLab Team / EntApps Team will remove the system from the tech stack.

Occasionally, systems listed in our tech stack will be deprecated. If a system is being offboarded (no longer used or being replaced), please submit a Remove a System request through the Tech Stack Update workflow in HelpLab.

As part of this process, the Business Owner must collaborate with Legal and IT Compliance to ensure that all data deletion and contractual requirements are completed according to the vendor agreement. Once all steps are validated, the system will be removed from the Tech Stack through the HelpLab workflow.

Other Related Processes

Please review our Access Requests handbook page to find more information on how to request access to systems. Choose the right template for your use case and follow the instructions.

Do you want to procure a new application/system? Visit the Procurement handbook page to find out more information on how to get started. Only applications which go through this process can be added to the tech stack.

Are you experiencing issues with an application/system? Visit our Tech Stack YAML and under Group Owner/Slack Channel , you can find out what Slack channel you need to reach out to request support.

Many applications listed in the Tech Stack are in-scope for regulatory or other compliance activities. For more information please reach out to the DRIs listed below:

FedRAMP: Corey Oas (@corey-oas), Security Compliance

GitLab SaaS SOC2: Liz Coleman (@lcoleman), Security Compliance

Updating the Offboarding Templates

The offboarding templates need to be updated when a new system is added to the Tech Stack in order for GitLab to be compliant and remove Team Members from systems once they leave GitLab. There are two different ways to update the offboarding templates.  Pick the option which best suits the usage of the new system. Once the MR is ready to be merged, tag the People Operations team - @gl-people-ops-team - for approval and merge.

Update the main Team Member Offboarding template.

The main template is divided into two sections.  The first section is the Offboarding Tasks section.  This section is applicable to all GitLab Team Members and is performed by the Manager, People, Business Technology, and Finance/Accounting teams.

The second section is the Tech Stack System Deprovisioning / Offboarding section.  Update this section with the new system being added to the Tech Stack.  Use the format below to make the update:

When finished, link the MR in the “Access Tasks” section of the ‘Tech Stack - Add New System’ MR.

Update the department-level Offboarding template within this directory.

Using the link above, locate the template of the department owning the new system and update it. Example: If only the Security team will have access to a new system, locate the Security template and add the new system to that template following this format:

- [ ]  @usernameofdeprovisioner @usernameofdeprovisioner2: Remove the team member from System name

When finished, link the MR in the “Access Tasks” section of the ‘Tech Stack - Add New System’ MR.

There are exceptions to adding technologies to the Tech Stack which are handled on a case-by-case basis.
